title: Contextuality from the Projector Overlap Matrix
authors: Ali Can Günhan, Semahi Serhat Aksoy, Zafer Gedik
abstract: We place several known indicators of Kochen--Specker contextuality -- the KCBS correlator $χ$, the contextual fraction $\CF$, the Shannon-entropic $n$-cycle inequality of Chaves and Fritz, and the operational commutator witness $D$ of Paper~I -- into a single projector-geometric framework organized around the overlap matrix $\Tcal_{ij} = d^{-1}\tr[(\hat P_i \hat Q_j)^2]$, where $\hat P_i$ and $\hat Q_j$ are the joint-eigenspace projectors of the two compatible observable pairs within a measurement context. The state-independent scalar content of $\Tcal$ is carried by two independent contractions: the mutual information energy $E = \sum_{ij}\Tcal_{ij}$ of Paper~I (equivalently, its logarithmic form $S_2 = -\log_2 E$), and the Maassen--Uffink extremal overlap $c_\MU = \max_{i,j}|\langle a_i,b_i | c_j,b_j\rangle|$. We prove that $S_2$ is non-increasing under coarse-graining, that $S_2(\Gcal) = 0$ is a necessary configuration-level condition for observable contextuality, and that the additive composition $S_2(\Gcal) = \sum_αS_2(\Gcal_α)$ is exact for the KCBS pentagon. We further show that in the spin-$1$ realization of the KCBS pentagon, a shared $m_s=0$ eigenstate in each context forces $c_\MU = 1$, rendering every Maassen--Uffink-type bound trivial -- a structural mechanism that makes explicit why outcome-entropic uncertainty relations based on $c_\MU$ are silent on KCBS contextuality, while $S_2 \approx 2.7266$~bits throughout. Applied to KCBS and CHSH, the framework identifies regimes in which every state-dependent witness considered here is silent yet $S_2(\Gcal) > 0$ by an amount set by the projector geometry alone.
summary: This paper introduces a new, state-independent indicator of quantum contextuality based on the geometric overlap of projectors. By analyzing the 'Projector Overlap Matrix,' the authors show that certain geometric properties can signal the presence of contextuality even when traditional, state-dependent witnesses fail to detect it. The work provides a unified view of several known contextuality measures and explains the failure of certain entropic uncertainty relations in specific spin-1 configurations.
main_problem: The paper seeks to unify various indicators of Kochen-Specker contextuality into a single framework based on a projector overlap matrix and investigates the relationship between state-independent geometric properties and state-dependent contextuality witnesses.
main_result: The authors establish that the quantity S2 (derived from the overlap matrix) is a necessary configuration-level condition for observable contextuality and can remain positive even when state-dependent witnesses are silent. They also identify a structural mechanism (shared eigenstates) that explains why certain entropic uncertainty relations fail to detect contextuality in the KCBS scenario.
method: The study uses a geometric framework involving a projector overlap matrix T, analyzing scalar contractions such as mutual information energy E and the Maassen-Uffink extremal overlap cMU. Analytical derivations and numerical evaluations are applied to KCBS and CHSH scenarios.
model_or_system: The research focuses on finite-dimensional Hilbert spaces, specifically the spin-1 KCBS pentagon (5-cycle) and the two-qubit CHSH 4-cycle configuration.
key_observables: KCBS correlator (chi), contextual fraction (CF), Shannon-entropic n-cycle inequality, operational commutator witness (D), and the projector overlap entropy (S2).
important_parameters: The mixing parameter p (controlling state purity), the Maassen-Uffink parameter cMU, and the angular configurations of observables in the CHSH and KCBS scenarios.
main_assumptions: The framework assumes the existence of compatible observable pairs within measurement contexts and focuses on specific n-cycle geometries.
figures_summary: The figures illustrate the relationship between different contextuality witnesses, the trade-off between Bell-inequality violation and entropic-inequality violation in the CHSH scenario, and the scaling of entropy for large n-cycles.
paper_structure: The paper introduces a unified framework using the overlap matrix, defines key scalar contractions, proves properties like monotonicity and additive exactness, and then applies the framework to the KCBS and CHSH scenarios to compare state-independent and state-dependent indicators.
why_interesting: This paper provides a deep geometric understanding of contextuality, which is fundamental to quantum information processing and the study of non-classical correlations in many-body systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

FIG. 2. Operational quantity D(GKCBS, ˆρ) of Paper I [16] on states outside the time-reversal-invariant family ˆρ(p) of Fig. 1, where D ≡0. Throughout, “TR” abbreviates time reversal; a state is TR-even if it is invariant under the antiunitary time- reversal operation, and TR-broken otherwise. (a) Pure-state sweep |ψ(s)⟩= cos s |0z⟩+ sin s |+1z⟩. The endpoints are qualitatively distinct: s = 0 is the TR-even state |0z⟩(open circle, D = 0) and s = π/2 is the TR-broken state |+1z⟩(filled circle, D ≈6.4984). Interior values 0 < s < π/2 correspond to superpositions of the two endpoints and are neither purely TR-even nor purely TR-broken; D rises across the sweep, with a mild non-monotonicity...
